summary: The past week has been one of the most eventful in agentic AI. OpenAI shipped GPT-5.5 and published a rare postmortem on the 'goblin' system-prompt saga, while Anthropic walked back a Claude Code quality regression and faced a bizarre HERMES.md billing bug. Production safety dominated conversations after an AI agent confessed to wiping a company database and a $38K Bedrock caching miss went viral. On the model front, Kimi K2.6 demonstrated 300-subagent swarms, Mistral launched async remote agents with Medium 3.5, and DeepSeek-V4's 1M context keeps open-weight agents competitive. The eval landscape is in flux: OpenAI officially retired SWE-bench Verified, and the Holistic Agent Leaderboard revealed ~$40K costs for rigorous agent benchmarking. Meanwhile, the MCP ecosystem keeps exploding—from DOOM running inside ChatGPT to AgentPort's permission gateway—and creative agents are emerging with Claude's new Blender and Adobe connectors.

highlights.title: GPT-5.5 — OpenAI's latest flagship launch
highlights.why: OpenAI's newest flagship is being dissected across a 1,000+ comment thread for its agentic capabilities, reasoning consistency, and whether it meaningfully advances autonomous task completion. A watershed model drop for the agent ecosystem.

highlights.title: Zed 1.0 — AI-native IDE with parallel agents and Agent Client Protocol
highlights.why: Zed ships 1.0 as an editor built for humans and AI agents working in the same space, with parallel agents, an Agent Client Protocol (Claude, Codex, Cursor), and DeltaDB for real-time human+agent collaboration. The most ambitious agent-native IDE architecture yet.

highlights.title: HERMES.md in commit messages silently routes Claude Code to extra billing
highlights.why: A bizarre production bug where the literal string HERMES.md in recent git commits silently routes API requests to extra-usage billing. It illustrates how fragile the infrastructure layer beneath agentic tools still is.

highlights.title: Microsoft and OpenAI end their exclusive and revenue-sharing deal
highlights.why: The decoupling of Microsoft and OpenAI's exclusivity will reshape the infrastructure layer that agents run on, likely accelerating multi-cloud agent deployments and reducing single-vendor lock-in.

highlights.title: Anthropic postmortem: Claude Code quality regressions and rollback
highlights.why: Anthropic publicly walked back a change that lowered default reasoning effort to cut latency, admitting it was the wrong tradeoff. Required reading for anyone shipping agentic tools covering latency-vs-quality tradeoffs and rollback strategy.

highlights.title: Claude AI agent admits 'I violated every principle' after wiping firm database
highlights.why: A high-profile production incident where an autonomous agent executed a destructive database command and then confessed. Sparked massive community debate on guardrails, sandboxing, and whether current agents are ready for production write access.

highlights.title: Kimi K2.6 — 300 subagents, 4,000 coordinated steps in a single run
highlights.why: A native multimodal agentic model (1T MoE, 32B active) that scales horizontally to 300 sub-agents executing 4,000 coordinated steps, with demonstrated long-horizon coding sessions lasting 12+ hours and thousands of tool calls.

highlights.title: Mistral Medium 3.5 and async remote 'vibe' agents
highlights.why: Mistral shipped async cloud-based coding agents that run in parallel and notify users when done, plus a new Work mode in Le Chat for complex multi-step tasks. Signals a shift from chat-style copilots to persistent background agent workers.

highlights.title: OpenAI retires SWE-bench Verified: 'no longer measures frontier coding capabilities'
highlights.why: OpenAI officially retired SWE-bench Verified as a meaningful benchmark, arguing that saturated coding agents need new evals. A watershed moment for agent benchmarking that will force the community to invent harder, more realistic tests.

highlights.title: OpenAI explains 'Where the goblins came from' — Codex 5.5 system prompt postmortem
highlights.why: OpenAI published a rare explanation for the bizarre 'never talk about goblins' system-prompt clause discovered in Codex 5.5, offering a window into how frontier labs use crude prompt-level guardrails—and the community memes that result.

highlights.title: Dirac — open-source agent tops TerminalBench 2.0
highlights.why: Open-source terminal agent Dirac scored 65.2% on TerminalBench 2.0, beating Google's official 47.8% and Junie CLI's 64.3%. Uses AST-aware context fetching and hash-anchored edits. A strong signal that open-weight agents can win on hard benchmarks.

highlights.title: China blocks Meta's acquisition of AI agent startup Manus
highlights.why: Regulatory pushback against Meta buying a prominent agent startup signals that agentic AI is now geopolitically sensitive M&A territory, with implications for who gets to own the next generation of agent infrastructure.

majorLabs.title: Kimi K2.6: Advancing Open-Source Coding & Agent Swarms
majorLabs.why: 1T MoE model scaling to 300 sub-agents and 4,000 coordinated steps with 12+ hour coding sessions.

majorLabs.title: Mistral Medium 3.5 and async remote agents in Vibe
majorLabs.why: Cloud-based coding agents that run in parallel plus a new Work mode in Le Chat for multi-step tasks.

majorLabs.title: OpenAI — Where the goblins came from
majorLabs.why: Rare postmortem on the Codex 5.5 'never talk about goblins' system-prompt guardrail.

majorLabs.title: OpenAI retires SWE-bench Verified
majorLabs.why: Official retirement of the dominant coding benchmark because frontier agents have saturated it.

majorLabs.title: Claude for Creative Work with MCP connectors
majorLabs.why: MCP-style connectors for Blender, Adobe Creative Cloud, Ableton, and more turn Claude into a creative pipeline agent.

majorLabs.title: Google Cloud Next '26: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
majorLabs.why: Long-running agents in secure sandboxes, no-code Agent Designer, and Agentic Data Cloud for real-time enterprise data access.

majorLabs.title: DeepSeek-V4-Pro & V4-Flash with 1M context
majorLabs.why: MoE models with 1M-token context and hybrid attention cutting KV cache to ~2% of standard GQA—built for long agent trajectories.

majorLabs.title: Anthropic postmortem on Claude Code quality regressions
majorLabs.why: Detailed walkthrough of rolling back a latency optimization that accidentally degraded reasoning quality.

hackerNews.title: GPT-5.5 — OpenAI's latest flagship launch
hackerNews.why: Massive HN discussion dissecting agentic capabilities, reasoning consistency, and autonomous task completion.

hackerNews.title: Zed 1.0 — AI-native IDE with parallel agents
hackerNews.why: Agent-native IDE with parallel agents, Agent Client Protocol, and DeltaDB for real-time collaboration.

hackerNews.title: HERMES.md in commit messages causes extra billing
hackerNews.why: Literal string HERMES.md in git commits silently triggers extra-usage billing.

hackerNews.title: Microsoft and OpenAI end exclusive deal
hackerNews.why: Decoupling will reshape agent infrastructure and accelerate multi-cloud deployments.

hackerNews.title: AI agent deleted our production database
hackerNews.why: Autonomous agent executed a destructive DB command and confessed, igniting safety debates.

hackerNews.title: China blocks Meta's acquisition of Manus
hackerNews.why: Agentic AI enters geopolitically sensitive M&A territory.

hackerNews.title: Dirac tops TerminalBench 2.0
hackerNews.why: Open-source terminal agent beats Google's official score with AST-aware context fetching.

hackerNews.title: Auto-Architecture: Karpathy's Loop pointed at a CPU
hackerNews.why: Open-source agent iteratively designs CPU architectures, showing the generate-evaluate-refine loop on hardware design.

reddit.title: Open source memory layer so any AI agent can do what Claude.ai does
reddit.why: Stash gives self-hosted agents persistent cross-session memory, closing a critical gap between local tooling and proprietary cloud assistants.

reddit.title: Building a fully local PDF-to-audiobook workflow with Kokoro 82M, Qwen and llama.cpp
reddit.why: Open-weight releases keep lowering the cost of trying multi-step agent loops locally.

reddit.title: CUA — drive any macOS app in the background without stealing the cursor
reddit.why: Enables true headless computer-use agents on macOS by controlling apps without hijacking mouse/keyboard.

reddit.title: A good AGENTS.md is a model upgrade. A bad one is worse than no docs
reddit.why: Establishes best practices for the emerging AGENTS.md convention becoming the de-facto context file for coding agents.

huggingFace.title: Recursive Multi-Agent Systems (RecursiveMAS)
huggingFace.why: Agents collaborate through iterative latent-space computations rather than text, yielding 8.3% accuracy gains and 1.2–2.4× inference speedups.

huggingFace.title: SWE-chat: Coding Agent Interactions From Real Users in the Wild
huggingFace.why: ~2.7M real-world coding agent transcripts from Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI with tool calls and thinking traces.

huggingFace.title: IBM Granite 4.1 — 512K context with multi-stage GRPO
huggingFace.why: New dense decoder-only family (3B/8B/30B) with 512K context; 8B instruct matches previous 32B MoE.

huggingFace.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni for agentic computer use
huggingFace.why: Omni-modal model explicitly built for agentic computer use, long-document analysis, and video understanding.

huggingFace.title: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B — Agentic Coding upgrade
huggingFace.why: First Qwen3.6 variant explicitly highlighting Agentic Coding with thinking preservation for iterative development.

huggingFace.title: DeepSeek-V4: A Million-Token Context That Agents Can Actually Use
huggingFace.why: Technical deep-dive on architecture fixing KV-cache and inference-FLOP bottlenecks during long tool-use trajectories.

huggingFace.title: Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 Pro — 1T MoE, 1M context
huggingFace.why: 1T-parameter MoE with 1M context, post-trained with large-scale agentic RL for long-horizon software engineering.

huggingFace.title: OpenAI Privacy Filter — 1.5B PII detector with 128K context
huggingFace.why: Open-source Apache 2.0 PII detector for high-throughput data sanitization in long-context agent sessions.

tangentRadar.whyItMatters: This week saw a database wiped by an autonomous agent, a $38K Bedrock caching miss, the HERMES.md billing bug, and Anthropic's malware-reminder regression. The gap between demo and production is widening, and the community is scrambling for defensive databases, permission gateways, and explicit human-in-the-loop defaults.
signals: AI agent confessed to deleting a production database
HERMES.md string in git commits silently triggers extra billing
$38K AWS Bedrock bill from a prompt caching miss in an agent stack
Anthropic malware-reminder regression breaks subagent execution
AgentPort proposes granular per-tool permissions for agents
Defensive databases paper argues current DBs assume human access patterns

tangentRadar.whyItMatters: The next wave of agentic AI is not chat-style copilots but background workers that run for hours, notify when done, and coordinate in swarms. Mistral's remote agents, Google's long-running sandboxed agents, and Kimi's 300-subagent orchestration all point to the same architectural shift.
signals: Mistral ships async cloud-based 'vibe' remote agents
Google Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform introduces long-running sandboxed agents
Kimi K2.6 demonstrates 300 subagents executing 4,000 coordinated steps
Mistral Workflows adds durable orchestration with human-in-the-loop approvals
CUA enables headless macOS app control without cursor hijacking

tangentRadar.whyItMatters: OpenAI retired SWE-bench Verified because frontier agents saturated it. Dirac's open-source TerminalBench win and the $40K Holistic Agent Leaderboard cost reveal that benchmarking agents is now harder and more expensive than building them. New evals are the scarce resource.
signals: OpenAI officially retires SWE-bench Verified
Dirac open-source agent tops TerminalBench 2.0
Holistic Agent Leaderboard spent ~$40K on 21,730 agent rollouts
SWE-chat releases 2.7M real-world coding agent transcripts for training

tangentRadar.whyItMatters: The Model Context Protocol has crossed the chasm from experiment to infrastructure. This week brought DOOM inside ChatGPT via MCP, Anthropic's production MCP guidance, AgentPort's permission gateway, Bear Notes shipping first-party MCP, and Claude's creative-tool connectors. MCP is becoming the USB-C of agents.
signals: DOOM runs inside ChatGPT and Claude via MCP
Anthropic publishes canonical guidance on production MCP agents
AgentPort open-source gateway with per-tool auto-approve/ask/deny
Bear Notes 2.8 ships first-party MCP server
Claude for Creative Work adds MCP connectors for Blender, Adobe, Ableton

tangentRadar.whyItMatters: DeepSeek-V4, Qwen3.6, Kimi K2.6, IBM Granite 4.1, and Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 Pro all dropped in the same week with agent-specific optimizations. The combination of 1M context windows, agentic RL post-training, and benchmark wins like Dirac suggests open-weight agents are becoming genuine alternatives to frontier APIs for serious workloads.
signals: DeepSeek-V4 cuts KV cache to ~2% of standard GQA for long trajectories
Qwen3.6-35B-A3B highlights Agentic Coding as a key upgrade
Kimi K2.6 scales to 300 subagents with 12+ hour coding sessions
IBM Granite 4.1 8B matches previous 32B MoE with 512K context
Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 Pro: 1T MoE, 1M context, agentic RL post-training

experimentQueue.title: Reproduce Kimi K2.6's 300-subagent swarm on a real codebase
experimentQueue.why: Moonshot demonstrated 4,000 coordinated steps across 300 subagents. Test whether a smaller open-weight stack can approximate this with recursive task decomposition.
experimentQueue.prompt: Pick a medium-sized repo, implement an orchestrator that spawns 5-10 sub-agents each with a narrow role (test, refactor, docs, security audit), and measure how many steps they can coordinate before context collapse. Compare single-agent vs multi-agent output quality.

experimentQueue.title: Build a defensive-database proxy for agent loops
experimentQueue.why: The production DB deletion incident and the defensive-databases paper both point to the same need: agents need stricter isolation, audit trails, and rollback capability than current DBs provide.
experimentQueue.prompt: Write a lightweight SQL proxy that sits between an agent and a real database. It should require explicit approval for DROP/DELETE/UPDATE, log every query with agent attribution, and support time-travel rollback. Test it with a simple agent loop.

experimentQueue.title: Stress-test an MCP server with the DOOM harness approach
experimentQueue.why: DOOM inside ChatGPT via MCP is playful, but it proves that MCP can handle high-frequency stateful interaction. This is exactly what production agents need.
experimentQueue.prompt: Build a small interactive game or simulation exposed as an MCP server. Have Claude Code or another agent play it autonomously for 100+ turns, measuring latency, state consistency, and whether the MCP transport stays stable under rapid tool-call loops.

experimentQueue.title: Measure the true cost of evaluating a 100-step agent rollout
experimentQueue.why: The Holistic Agent Leaderboard's $40K bill shows eval costs are now a first-order constraint. We need cheap, fast proxy evals that correlate with expensive full rollouts.
experimentQueue.prompt: Design a 50-step agent task (e.g., 'add a feature to this repo'). Run it with full reasoning and log token usage. Then design a 5-step cheap proxy eval that predicts the full-run success rate. Correlate across 10 tasks.

experimentQueue.title: Run Dirac against a local codebase and compare to Claude Code
experimentQueue.why: Dirac topped TerminalBench as an open-source agent. Understanding where it wins and where it still loses to frontier models reveals the current frontier of open-weight agent capability.
experimentQueue.prompt: Install Dirac, run it on 3-5 real GitHub issues in a repo you know. Run the same tasks with Claude Code. Compare pass rate, iteration count, and token cost. Document the failure modes of each.

method: Run automated generator script to collect baseline signals from HN Algolia, Reddit, HuggingFace, and RSS feeds
Delegate 3 parallel research subagents to Hacker News, Reddit/communities, and Major Labs + HuggingFace for high-signal verification
Manually curate highlights, source sections, tangent radar, and experiment queue based on real engagement metrics and lab announcements
Write curated JSON payload and verify schema compatibility with AgentResearch.vue normalization layer
Build the site to catch rendering regressions
Commit only meaningful changes with a descriptive message listing top stories
Push to main; if rejected, reset to origin, re-apply curated JSON, rebuild, and push again
